prompt:
We're standardizing how our report scripts talk to the metrics API and I have opinions, because the last attempt kept its token in a script-scope variable and the moment one runbook needed two accounts at once everything cross-talked. So: session state is a VALUE. You create it, you pass it around, functions read and update it. Nothing tucked away in `$script:` anywhere — the test actually greps for that.

Deliverable is `tokenpass.ps1` with two functions:

`New-ApiSession -BaseUrl <u> -Account <a> -Secret <s>` returns the session (a hashtable) with at least `State`, `Token`, and `History`. Fresh sessions are `State = 'anonymous'`, no token, and `History` = the list of states so far, i.e. just `anonymous`. No network here.

`Invoke-ApiGet -Session <s> -Path </data/...>` returns the raw response body string. Life cycle:
- First use of an anonymous session logs in first: `POST <base>/login` with JSON `{"account":..,"secret":..}`. On 200 stash the token, state goes `active` (History records every state change). On 403 state goes `rejected` and throw `tokenpass: login rejected for account <a>`.
- Every data call sends `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. 200 → return the body.
- On a 401, assume the token aged out: state `renewing`, log in again (once!), state `active`, and replay the SAME request. If the replay 401s too, state `rejected`, throw `tokenpass: still unauthorized after re-login`. One re-login per call, never a loop.
- A rejected session stays dead: any further call throws `tokenpass: session rejected` without touching the network.
- Anything else (500 etc.) is NOT an auth problem: throw `tokenpass: unexpected status <code> from <path>` and leave the session state alone. Do not re-login on a 500 — that's how we got the login-storm incident.

Two sessions for different accounts must be usable interleaved from the same runspace without stepping on each other.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_tokenpass.ps1`. It runs a local mock of the API and pins the full request log the server saw — including which bearer token went out on which call — plus your state histories, so the exact login/replay accounting matters. Hands off the test file and mock_authapi.py.
notes: 6P6 item 3: token-session client as a pinned state machine (anonymous/active/renewing/rejected via History), session state passed as data — enforced two ways: source scan bans $script:/$global:/New-Variable, and the interleaved alice+bob scenario pins per-session tokens in the server-side request log. 401-once semantics pinned by log (exactly one re-login then replay; /data/locked proves no loop and terminal rejected state); 500 pinned as fail-without-relogin (log shows zero /login) per the login-storm framing. Differs from py-sessionclient (cookie-jar Set-Cookie sessions, py) and py-tokenflow (OAuth2 token cache/refresh-guard, no replay pinning): this is bearer-token + explicit-state-as-data in pwsh with server-side log pinning. catalog wave6/6P6/3.
